page: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server powered by Intel Xeon 8375C processors. It features 128 vCPUs across 64 physical cores with a base clock speed of 3.5 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The hardware configuration includes 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing 8.0 GB of RAM per physical core. For local storage, the server is equipped with 7600 GB of NVMe SSD instance store volumes. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core and memory performance. The system achieved a strong multi-core CPU score of 124999.69 in stress-ng and a top-tier PassMark CPU score of 87984.85, alongside a top-tier score of 114648.53 in CPU encryption tests. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, reaching a strong read-only small block cached speed of 6698.67 GB/sec and a large block uncached write speed of 133.36 GB/sec. In database and application-level benchmarks, the server demonstrated strong capabilities with 107.97 million operations per second in Redis SET operations and a strong PassMark database operations score of 30214.15. Single-core performance remains average, as shown by a Geekbench single-core score of 1682.0.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r6idn.metal represents a high-density resource profile that consolidates massive memory capacity, high-speed local NVMe storage, and high-bandwidth networking into a single bare-metal instance. This eliminates virtualization overhead and makes it highly suitable for large-scale, memory-intensive workloads. The server is optimized for enterprise databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, large-scale data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal, memory-optimized server designed for resource-intensive enterprise workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores running at 3.5 GHz, it features 1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The instance delivers high-speed networking with a 200 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Performance benchmarks highlight strong multi-core capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 124999.69, and exceptional memory throughput, reaching 6698.67 GB/sec for cached small blocks. Database operations are highly efficient, supported by a Redis SET performance of 107.97 million operations per second. This bare-metal configuration is optimized for large-scale databases, in-memory data stores, and high-throughput data processing applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal server with 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB RAM, 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage, and 200 Gbps bandwidth, optimized for large-scale databases and in-memory workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services r6idn.metal is a bare-metal server featuring 128 vCPUs, 1024 GB DDR4 RAM, and 7600 GB NVMe SSD storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-performance bare-metal platform combining massive memory capacity, fast NVMe storage, and high-speed networking for demanding enterprise databases.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8375C processor with 128 vCPUs and 64 physical cores
1024.0 GB of DDR4 memory and 7600 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
High-speed network connectivity with 200 Gbps baseline bandwidth
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read/write bandwidth
Optimized for large-scale databases, Redis caching, and data processing
